heading: Postage unpaid on deposited mail matter
label: 1725 Postage unpaid on deposited mail matter
text: Whoever knowingly and willfully deposits any mailable matter such as statements of accounts, circulars, sale bills, or other like matter, on which no postage has been paid, in any letter box established, approved, or accepted by the Postal Service for the receipt or delivery of mail matter on any mail route with intent to avoid payment of lawful postage thereon, shall for each such offense be fined under this title.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 321a (, ).May 7, 1934, ch. 220, § 248 Stat. 667Reference to persons aiding or assisting was struck out as unnecessary since such persons are made principals by .section 2 of this titleMinor verbal changes were made.
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $300”.Pub. L. 103–3221970— substituted “Postal Service” for “Postmaster General”.Pub. L. 91–375

Effective Date of 1970 AmendmentAmendment by effective within 1 year after , on date established therefor by Board of Governors of United States Postal Service and published by it in Federal Register, see , set out as an Effective Date note preceding , Postal Service.Pub.
